Goan people in my neighborhood are good, but non goans in my neighborhood are jealous and annoying (karnataka people). But we love the locals. Fight with us because when it rains some water falls on their house etc small bullshit.

responses.loveText: wonderful, quiet neighborhood that is almost perfect—if only the local authorities would do their job and keep the infrastructure up to par.
responses.commentsText: gutters and 24 hours water and underground electricity was promised still waiting for it and if they give 24 hours water please give meter at the same time otherwise people will misuse it roads management should be done sweeper should be appointed

responses.roadComment: Even the most prominent road, like MG Road is full of bumps and pots.

responses.loveText: It’s close to the beach, great location.

responses.roadComment: Patchwork and repair is done that lasts from monsoon to monsoon. Once a year sewage pipes are clean and garbage piled on the edge, never again to be removed. Monsoon washes it back into the drains - corruption issue.
responses.loveText: No sound pollution, less air pollution due to greenery, large open space for me to have a kitchen garden, family and support system in the area, only 1 entry gate so safe for kids to play.
responses.commentsText: If only we as human beings and citizens could change and stop failing each other. The government cannot do everything for us. We are the most unhygienic people and we make our areas like open bathroom spitoons. Traffic jams also due to green and a lack of pride in their jobs.

responses.electricityComment: Decent but gets disrupted during rainy days
responses.roadComment: Absolute worst, half dug up roads, loose gravel all around , dust
responses.safetyComment: Lot secure than most neighbourhoods
responses.loveText: Greenary
responses.commentsText: Political hoardings everywhere, even the bus stops have politician face on it and on parks every bench is carved with politician name who is a local mla, I have raised rti but no response

responses.electricityComment: Occasionally powercuts happen, especially during monsoon
responses.roadComment: The quality of the roads is okay but there is too much traffic everywhere, constant honking and rule breaking is very common here. There is construction going on everywhere which has ruined the air quality and has increased the traffic severally
responses.safetyComment: There are several areas in the neighborhood where class 1 drugs such as weed maybe sold, the authorities already know about it but it still is rampant. Most of these people are tippers and work for the police.
responses.businessComment: Homestly, more cleanliness and accesible roads would be amazing, there are normal street vendors and vegetable vendors that take 40% of the road in some areas, a fixed area for this would be better
responses.loveText: I have grown up in this neighborhood growing up it was very united community, people from all walks of life and religion would coexist. But lately there have been instances where communal politics has been seen and because of that certian incidents have also happened which have been somehow stopped by the locals to not make it a huge deal.
responses.commentsText: The water quality we get is quite poor, for eg my Buidlung has BMC supply, and a Borewell supply which we have created. For the past 6 months we are getting water (BMC) that is smelly, and seems like sewage water, on days where we do get it, it is very minimal. If we rely on borewell we have been getting less and less because someone in the area has started a water tanmer business and is oulling from the groundwater supply which is lowering the water level for everyone.

responses.roadComment: Roads are getting damaged by construction of houses , but after construction both owner nor the government fixes the road may after few years they deside to fill the pothole but still will be like a patch not smooth as before
responses.businessComment: Parking spaces and good footpath without vehicle and blocked by random stuffs
responses.loveText: It's very central location to Bangalore but still not at all crowded it's like peace but as central you can get before evening gets crowded

responses.electricityComment: Supply is good, throughout the year. However, the electrical poles are not maintained well. It is quite risky at some places.
responses.roadComment: They build concrete roads every few years, for drainage, and they say, they forgot install pipes and they build again, but the build quality is bad.
responses.commentsText: There is no drainage system in my neighbourhood yet. All my lifetime, we still have open gutters. However recently they installed drainage system pipes inside the roads, but the pipes they installed are so low quality, and the size of the pipe is just 6 inches. I wonder, how is that gonna hold up.

responses.loveText: Its safer and cleaner than surrounding area. Market is close by and work is approachable. Otherwise i seldom leave house

responses.loveText: Community, still keeping what somewhat village community in a urban/semi urban town-ish village midst in middle of city!

responses.electricityComment: Nowadays a lot of random powercuts are observed
responses.roadComment: It's not worth calling them roads especially when you've lived abroad where roads are designed... Let's call these alleyways always under construction and still have potholes and uneven levelling
responses.loveText: Compared to most countries abroad and most other parts of India, I feel extremely safe in my neighborhood and largely the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, even if my wife were to travel late in the night alone, touch wood safety hasn't been a big concern so far.
responses.commentsText: It's also been BJP and RSS 'Balekilla' with decades of their stronghold  and cultural influence.

responses.electricityComment: The area where I live doesn't still has government connection reached so a post property owner of this area installed a transformer lf his own money and charges extra too extra for electricity connection about 50000 for it's availability at your house followed up by bills later.
responses.roadComment: It's not even built it's a raw (kacchi road here in my society) It's a undeveloped society but roads are needed. We personally had to made dumb some damur material to make this raw road lil fine as in rainy days it's because bizzare.
responses.safetyComment: Lack of safety regular theft take place here and not safe to go out even cases of kidnapping and murders are pretty often nearby
responses.loveText: It's not too populated yet so i get some peace here although it's not gonna last more than 5 years many apartments are being built so it's gonna be overpopulated soon 🤧🤧🥺
responses.commentsText: Some parks and even local shops are lacking here I've to go pretty far to buy stationary amd other stuff there's no development here no safety no roads and electricity is cutted off every night for too long man we need to sleep after long working days and lack of safety people lacking civic sense eve teasing is too much commenting is too much I've been inappropriately touched twice in public places here been bad  commented tons of times .

responses.loveText: That we don't care about educating ourselves citizen participation, and we love to compain and blame than take action
responses.commentsText: A strong community that can have deep roots in local governing body is missing since the last 20 years in 560075

responses.electricityComment: Very frequent power cuts some times
responses.roadComment: Illegal encroachment in front of my society itself, the person is not allowing to build roads
responses.commentsText: Please help in building proper roads

responses.electricityComment: Ridiculously expensive. Senseless usage policy. Maharashtra (MSEDCL) probably is the most expensive DISCOM in the country for residential electricity. You pay almost double with every slab in and around KDMC(my municipality). And the charges for usage from 0-100 would directly double if you used 101 units on a certain month. Same with 100-300, 300-500 and 500+. Charges don't get separated slab-wise!
responses.roadComment: I know roads in entire Maharashtra are pretty bad.. But I don't know what it is about Mumbai, Thane, Kalyan, Dombivali, Ulhasnagar. Our roads are really badly planned and horribly made. The upkeep is an excuse for contractors to make money. Speed breakers hit cars with very high ground clearence. Which leads to entire city buying taller and bigger cars.
responses.safetyComment: There are entitled teenager of a certain political and community classes which behave like the own the streets, drive horribly, Dark out the glasses. Cops do nothing about them.
responses.businessComment: Road, Walkability, Garbage. City itself is a hazard for the general populous.
responses.loveText: My closer neighbourhood has good people, well educated and roads have been recently concretised.
responses.commentsText: Make solar net metering easier in Maharashtra! Why is this not a priority?

responses.electricityComment: There are power cuts for multiple hours during peak summer. This happens about 5-6 times every month.
responses.roadComment: They do clean and maintain the roads well, but the footpaths aren't as well maintained.
responses.safetyComment: Kamothe is a very calm town overall, I've never faced or heard of anyone facing any safety issue, but streetlights do need a bit of improvement.
responses.loveText: People are generally nice, there's good connectivity to Mumbai, Pune and Thane. Good restaurants and snack corners, multiple gyms, pretty close to NMI airport, multiple hospitals and banks close by, and comparatively affordable housing than the rest of Navi Mumbai
responses.commentsText: They NEED TO have better cleanliness and larger public parks, the current ones are crowded, and better management of traffic is required too.

responses.electricityComment: These days power cuts have become frequent. Another problem is MSEB officers whom we called for damaged meter asked us to come there physically to file complain but upon going there, perhaps looking at my privileged face, they conveyed that I didn't have to come.
responses.roadComment: There are cement roads everywhere but strips have been excavated for water pipeline have not been repaired.
responses.safetyComment: People drive very fast in the residential place. Some follow girls on bikes, some on feet. Girls playing badminton or merely strolling out of their houses gets honked at or they maybe weird noises. Some affray occurs once a while. Drunkard pass out on the streets or worse in the gate.
responses.loveText: Market places are very close.
responses.commentsText: Our corporator who recently hit elected, he has worked very well for us in the past, on the election day there was an affray on the polling station as people from rural side came to vote in corporation election. It was alleged that around 700 people were brought from rural side. Also whole election was speculated to be rigged as 45% of population voted i.e. 5.5 lakhs but more than 2 lakh people voted after 17:00 and didn't see voting going on after time.

responses.electricityComment: It’s more of a rural township in wester ghats. So there’d be constant power cuts. Situation is worse as we move interior
responses.loveText: Comparatively clean air and less pollution

responses.electricityComment: There are frequent power cuts, especially in summers, in mae of - preparing for monsoons - but the first rains and boom, no power! What preparation!

responses.businessComment: Better road with footpath, and parking. Proper planning.
responses.loveText: It's quiet and less traffic. So long!

Mission bhagiratha - every home gets water connectioin for free sucessful. Mission kakatiya - i dont know what happened. mission haritha haram - successful hope govt plan fro 2.0, kg to pg flop. If possible i request to make a video on TIMS telangana.
